ai_summary: Here's an executive summary of the provided financial document:  Bajaj Auto Limited reported a strong Q1 FY27, with consolidated revenue from operations increasing 65.1% year-over-year to ₹21,688.83 crore and profit after tax growing 45.9% to ₹3,225.63 crore, driven by robust automotive unit sales. The company successfully completed a material ₹5,632.80 crore share buyback post-quarter, which will impact future capital structure and EPS, though net profit saw a modest sequential decline quarter-on-quarter primarily due to higher tax expenses. A significant red flag is the unquantified but potentially material obligation related to new End-of-Life Vehicle rules, posing future financial uncertainty.
